behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is SUM range-evaluation logic inside formula evaluation. MOCK-MISS: this behavior does not require multi-module handoff beyond one evaluator/function implementation. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: behavior text is only about computing the sum correctly, not recalculation propagation or persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an evaluator/unit fixture with A1..A10 numeric values, evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)`, assert exact numeric result (including mixed ints/floats case). No filesystem/network/process dependencies.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff: reference resolution + dependency evaluation + arithmetic evaluator. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only arithmetic or only parser can miss cascade bugs. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required, so not System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text is about cascaded formula correctness, not real infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook/sheet in-process, set A1 value, B1 formula `=A1*2`, C1 formula `=B1+1`, evaluate C1, assert final value. Verify dependency chain resolution occurs across components, with no external I/O.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is an interaction between dependency graph construction and evaluator execution state. MOCK-MISS: single-function tests can miss recursion/visited-state bugs at component boundaries. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text targets logical cycle handling, not infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: In one in-process workbook, set A1 `=B1` and B1 `=A1`, trigger evaluation, assert circular-reference error type/code and no infinite loop/timeout. Optionally include a non-cyclic control case in same suite.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is built-in function semantics for exact-match lookup and not-found handling. MOCK-MISS: no required multi-module interaction beyond function logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: behavior text is function correctness, not dependency propagation/orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke VLOOKUP evaluator with deterministic table fixtures: one key present and one absent. Assert returned value for match and expected error token for miss.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is expression evaluation/branching semantics for nested IF. MOCK-MISS: no cross-module boundary is required to expose incorrect branch logic. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: behavior text is isolated function expression correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate nested IF formula with A1 values spanning branches (e.g., 5, 15, 25). Assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high` respectively within evaluator unit test fixture.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is CONCATENATE function string-assembly rules (separator + empty handling). MOCK-MISS: does not require module interaction to fail. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: behavior text focuses on function output formatting semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run CONCATENATE formula/function with fixture cells including empty/null values and explicit separator, assert resulting string omits empty-cell placeholders and preserves separator placement.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-sheet reference resolution across workbook/sheet namespace + evaluator. MOCK-MISS: unit-only tests of parser or arithmetic can miss sheet-binding bugs. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text limits scope to in-memory sheet existence/resolution.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2 in-process; set Sheet2!A1, place `=Sheet2!A1+1` in Sheet1, evaluate and assert value. Include check that resolver binds to correct sheet context.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit orchestration/state handoff across dependency graph, invalidation, and recomputation modules. MOCK-MISS: isolated unit tests can pass while propagation order fails across components. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text concerns internal topological recalculation, not OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build dependency graph with multiple levels/branches (e.g., A1 -> B1/B2 -> C1), evaluate baseline, mutate A1, trigger recompute, assert all dependent values update correctly and in dependency-safe order (no stale intermediate outputs).

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary spans undo/redo history state + formula storage + dependency recalculation. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing undo stack alone can miss restoration/recompute mismatches. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text is internal state restoration, not infrastructure interaction.
behaviors.test_description: In an in-process workbook, set formula and dependent cells, capture computed state, edit formula to change outputs, execute undo, assert original formula text restored and dependent computed values exactly match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Minimum real-failure boundary is serializer/deserializer + workbook model round-trip; local filesystem use is sufficient and fits Integration per level defs. REAL-INFRA check: no network/subprocess required; OS-specific FS behavior is not the asserted failure mode. MOCK-HIDE: mocking persistence could hide format bugs, so use real local temp file. Rejected System: behavior text targets round-trip data fidelity/precision, not OS event/timing/process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with formulas and floating-point edge values, save to a real temp file in custom binary format, reload via normal load path, assert formula text and evaluated/raw values match (with strict or ulp-bounded float equality as spec requires).
